EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Florence approached the southeastern North Carolina coast in September 2018 after reaching peak intensity as a Category 4 hurricane over the open Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Florence made landfall near Wrightsville Beach, North Carolina, on September 14, 2018 as a Category 1 hurricane with maximum sustained winds near 80 knots. The slow-moving storm produced catastrophic and record-breaking freshwater flooding across eastern North Carolina over the following days.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Dorian devastated the Bahamas as a Category 5 hurricane before turning north along the U.S. East Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Dorian made landfall over Cape Hatteras, North Carolina, on September 6, 2019 as a Category 1 hurricane. Storm surge and wind damage affected the Outer Banks, and Ocracoke Island experienced severe flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Irene tracked up the U.S. East Coast in late August 2011 after peaking as a Category 3 hurricane near the Bahamas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Irene made landfall near Cape Lookout, North Carolina, on August 27, 2011 as a Category 1 hurricane. Widespread wind damage and storm surge flooding occurred across the Carteret County coast and the Pamlico Sound.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Onshore flow ahead of Hurricane Florence raised water levels along the Carteret County coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Significant storm surge of 4 to 6 feet was observed along the Bogue Banks and inner Carteret County shoreline as Florence approached, inundating low-lying roads in Morehead City and Beaufort.
